item.title: Too late to operate? Early surgical assessment in the era of advanced medical therapy for Crohn's disease.
item.description: Colorectal disease : the official journal of the Association of Coloproctology of Great Britain and Ireland - The expanding availability of advanced medical therapies has profoundly reshaped the management of Crohn's disease. However, an unintended consequence may be the progressive deferral of surgical in...

item.title: Staged Surgical Management of a Colosplenic Fistula Caused by Locally Advanced Splenic-Flexure Adenocarcinoma.
item.description: Clinical case reports - Colosplenic fistula is a rare complication most commonly associated with colonic lymphoma, adenocarcinoma, and Crohn's disease. In unstable and septic patients, a staged strategy consisting of init...

item.title: Dynamic profiling in inflammatory Bowel disease: A manifesto for personalized care.
item.description: Autoimmunity reviews - Review - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), encompassing Crohn's disease and ulcerative colitis, is a heterogeneous chronic condition whose clinical management has been advanced by the availability of molecu...

item.title: Editorial: Beyond Resection-Rethinking Postoperative Management in Colonic Crohn's Disease. Authors' Reply.

item.description: Drug discoveries & therapeutics - Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is a digestive system disorder characterized by chronic recurrent inflammation, primarily including ulcerative colitis (UC) and Crohn's disease (CD). Although moder...

item.title: Myelin-oligodendrocyte glycoprotein antibody-associated disease in Crohn's disease: an exceptional neurological manifestation.
item.description: Arab journal of gastroenterology : the official publication of the Pan-Arab Association of Gastroenterology - Case report - Myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein antibody-associated disease (MOGAD) is a rare demyelinating syndrome of the central nervous system (CNS). item.title: Butyrate in ulcerative colitis and pouchitis; friend or foe?
item.description: The British journal of nutrition - Review - It is widely known that the short chain fatty acid butyrate, generated mainly through fermentation of dietary fibre by anaerobic bacteria, has beneficial effects on health. Less widely known is tha...

item.title: THE RISK OF INFLAMMATORY BOWEL DISEASE IS INCREASED IN CELIAC DISEASE BUT NOT IN DERMATITIS HERPETIFORMIS.

item.title: Macrophage MERTK restrains GRAMD1A-driven mitochondrial oxysterol trafficking to limit colitis.

item.title: CrohnTwin-X: An explainable digital twin framework for predicting postoperative recurrence in Crohn's disease using multi-omics and clinical features.
item.description: Computer methods and programs in biomedicine - Crohn's disease (CD) is a chronic inflammatory disorder with a high rate of postoperative recurrence, posing major challenges for long-term management. Conventional clinical indices have limited ca...

item.title: A model for Crohn's disease post-ileal resection fibrosis development using human ileal enteroids and myofibroblasts.
item.description: Frontiers in physiology - Fibrosis and inflammation frequently emerge soon after ileal resection for strictures in Crohn's disease (CD). This study examined the profibrotic and proinflammatory effects of conditioned media f...

item.title: Pathophysiological mechanisms and clinical management of type 2 diabetes mellitus complicated with anal fistula.
item.description: Frontiers in surgery - Review - The comorbidity of type 2 diabetes mellitus (T2DM) and anal fistula is a prevalent global clinical challenge. Anal fistula is the second most common anorectal disease, with an incidence 1.81-2.01 t...
